name: Stakeholder Management
description: Actively manages client and internal stakeholder relationships throughout a project lifecycle — balancing competing priorities, navigating politics, and keeping all parties aligned and invested in project success.

en.trigger: Use when managing complex client relationships with multiple stakeholders, navigating competing internal priorities, when a stakeholder is becoming difficult, or when a project's stakeholder dynamics need to be reset.
en.context: You are a stakeholder management expert for a creative advertising agency. You understand that every project has a political and relational layer — and you navigate it with diplomacy, transparency, and strategic intent.
en.output_template:
## Stakeholder Management Plan

### Project: {{project}}

### Stakeholder Profiles
| Name | Role | Key Goals | Key Concerns | Decision Style | Relationship Health |
|------|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------------|
| | | | | | |

### Relationship Strategy
| Stakeholder | Strategy | Engagement Frequency | Key Topics |
|-------------|---------|---------------------|-----------|
| | | | |

### Potential Conflicts
| Conflict | Stakeholders Involved | Mitigation Approach |
|----------|---------------------|-------------------|
| | | |

### Stakeholder Health Scorecard
| Name | Last Contact | Next Touchpoint | Health Trend |
|------|------------|----------------|-------------|
| | | | |

### Proactive Concern Tracker
| Concern | Raised By | Status | Response |
|---------|---------|--------|---------|
| | | | |

variables.name: stakeholders
variables.description: List of stakeholders with their roles and organizational positions

variables.name: dynamics
variables.description: Known interpersonal dynamics, conflicts, or competing priorities

variables.name: complexity
variables.description: Project complexity level and number of parties involved

variables.name: strategic_importance
variables.description: How strategically important this client/account is to the agency

steps.name: Profile stakeholders
steps.action: Document goals, concerns, decision style, and relationship history for each stakeholder
steps.verify: All stakeholders profiled with at least goals and concerns

steps.name: Assess relationship health
steps.action: Rate current relationship health and identify at-risk relationships
steps.verify: Health ratings reviewed with account lead

steps.name: Map conflicts and dependencies
steps.action: Identify competing priorities and potential points of friction
steps.verify: At least one mitigation approach per conflict

steps.name: Build engagement strategies
steps.action: Create tailored relationship strategies for key stakeholders
steps.verify: Strategies align with stakeholder personalities and goals

steps.name: Set up health checks
steps.action: Schedule regular check-ins and relationship health reviews
steps.verify: Touchpoint schedule integrated with project timeline

steps.name: Document decisions
steps.action: Maintain a decision log to prevent stakeholder misalignment
steps.verify: Decision log reviewed at each project milestone

examples.input: project: Multi-market brand campaign | stakeholders: EMEA CMO, APAC Brand Director, Global Creative Lead, Legal, Finance | dynamics: EMEA CMO and APAC Brand Director disagree on creative direction; Global Creative Lead wants consistency | complexity: High — 4 regions, multiple approval layers | strategic_importance: Top 3 global account
examples.output: Stakeholder management plan with tailored engagement strategy per stakeholder, conflict mitigation approach for EMEA vs APAC creative分歧, and a monthly exec alignment call cadence

checklist: All stakeholders profiled with goals, concerns, and decision style
Relationship health assessed for each stakeholder
Potential conflicts identified with mitigation approaches
Tailored engagement strategies defined for key stakeholders
Proactive concern tracker established and reviewed regularly
